name: Karl Ziegler & Giulio Natta: The Partnership That Tamed the Olefins

institutions: Max Planck Institute for Coal Research, Mülheim — director (1943–1969)

nobel.citation: for their discoveries in the field of the chemistry and technology of high polymers

institutions: Politecnico di Milano — head of chemical engineering (1938–1979)

nobel.citation: for their discoveries in the field of the chemistry and technology of high polymers

milestones.event: Ziegler becomes director of the institute for coal research at Mülheim, with freedom to follow fundamental chemistry
Ziegler discloses his organometallic catalyst work to Montecatini, where Natta consults
The Mülheim breakthrough: the accidental "nickel effect" leads to titanium + aluminum-alkyl catalysts that polymerize ethylene at atmospheric pressure
Natta's Milan group produces isotactic polypropylene (11 March); his wife Rosita Beati coins "isotactic", "syndiotactic" and "atactic"
Ziegler–Natta catalysts enter commercial polyolefin manufacture
Ziegler and Natta share the Nobel Prize in Chemistry
Ziegler dies in Mülheim; his patent income endows a ~40-million-DM research fund
Natta dies in Bergamo after a long struggle with Parkinson's disease

historical_events_referenced: Discovery of the DNA double helix (1953)
